metadata.name: Multi-Signal Comparator Knowledge Base
metadata.purpose: Determine when single signals beat ensembles vs when ensembles are superior; optimize confidence thresholds and conflict resolution

high_conviction_single.name: High Conviction Single Signal (No Ensemble Needed)
high_conviction_single.description: When a single signal has exceptional confluence and regime alignment, it often outperforms ensemble consensus
conditions: Signal confidence >= 80%
Signal is regime-aligned (e.g., trend signal in trend regime)
Setup is textbook/clean (multiple confluences)
Historical accuracy on this exact setup > 65%
Price action supports signal strongly
signal_examples: Trend agent says 'GO' with 90% confidence in trending market
Price breaks above key resistance with volume spike during trend regime
Support holds overnight after 3 tests; bounce next day
Volume spike 200%+ during directional move
why_single_beats_ensemble: Ensemble dilutes conviction by averaging disagreeing agents
Single signal captures pure directional insight without averaging down
Risk-adjusted returns higher on highest-confidence setups
Timing: Single can execute faster (no committee delays)
validation_rules: Price action must visually support signal
Historical backtest on exact setup required

gap_fill_single.name: Gap-Fill Pattern (Mechanical, High Accuracy Single)
gap_fill_single.description: Gap-fill patterns (70%+ accuracy) often work better as single signals; ensemble consensus on fade may be weaker than pattern confidence
conditions: Clear gap (> 0.5% from prior close)
Pattern is gap-fill (71% probability from historical data)
Time elapsed < 60 minutes from gap opening
No major conflicting news/catalyst
signal_examples: Gap down at premarket open; consolidation at high; fade as volume builds
Gap up overnight; institutional distribution visible; short signal
why_single_beats_ensemble: Gap-fill is mechanical pattern; ensemble complexity adds noise
Single pattern traders have validated this setup extensively (200+ samples)
Ensemble agents may disagree on fade vs breakout; pattern is clear

momentum_spike_single.name: Momentum Spike Single (Volume Confirmation)
momentum_spike_single.description: Volume spike + momentum extremes often work better as pure signal; ensemble may hedge too much
conditions: Volume spike >= 150% of 20-period average
Momentum (RSI, MACD) at extremes (> 75 or < 25)
Direction of spike aligned with trend (if trend regime)
ATR expanding (volatility supporting momentum)
signal_examples: Volume spike 250% during uptrend; momentum bullish extreme
Large institutional buy order (visible order book); momentum confirms
why_single_beats_ensemble: Momentum spike is mechanical; ensemble adds subjectivity
Institutional volume reads faster than LLM analysis
Pure momentum traders have edge in execution speed

macro_event_single.name: Macro Event / News Catalyst (Time-Limited Single)
macro_event_single.description: Known macro events (earnings, FOMC, employment data) have mechanical outcomes; ensemble may overthink
conditions: Major macro event occurred (FOMC, Jobs, earnings, etc.)
Historical precedent on similar events clear
Event outcome known (e.g., surprise up/down)
Time-sensitive (only valid 30-60min post-event)
signal_examples: FOMC announced 50bp hike (hawkish surprise); crypto crashes; short signal
Better-than-expected jobs data; risk-on sentiment; long signal
why_single_beats_ensemble: Macro event outcomes mechanical; historical precedent clear
Ensemble agents may waffle on uncertain event interpretation
Single thesis (event is hawkish = sell) is purer

technical_reversal_single.name: Technical Reversal (Clear Pattern, Fast Entry)
technical_reversal_single.description: Classic technical reversals (engulfing, pin bar, hammer) often better as pure pattern signal; ensemble consensus may dilute them
conditions: Textbook reversal candle (engulfing, pin bar, hammer)
Reversal at key level (support/resistance, MA)
Prior trend was strong (minimum 3 bars same direction)
Volume confirming reversal
signal_examples: Engulfing candle at key support after downtrend; bullish reversal
Hammer candle at resistance after uptrend; bearish reversal
why_single_beats_ensemble: Technical reversal is pattern-based; ensemble agents may hesitate on thesis change
Ensemble may wait for 2-3 candle confirmation; single acts faster

conflicting_signals_ensemble.name: Conflicting Single Signals (Ensemble Superior)
conflicting_signals_ensemble.description: When individual signals conflict or provide contradictory advice, ensemble voting/consensus superior to trusting single signal
conditions: 2+ strategies give conflicting signals (go vs skip vs flip)
Regime classification ambiguous (25-75% confidence)
Price action unclear (inside bar, doji, indecision)
Market transitioning between regimes
signal_examples: Trend strategy says GO (breakout attempt); Mean reversion says SKIP (overbought)
Regime agent unsure (50% trend, 50% range); Trade agent hesitates
Volume mixed; price action conflicted
why_ensemble_beats_single: Ensemble voting gives consensus when signals conflict
Ensemble can identify regime transitioning (multiple confirmations)
Single signal in conflict = higher false positive rate
Ensemble risk agent can flag over-leverage risks single might miss
conflicting_signals_ensemble.decision_rule: If signal conflict detected, defer to ensemble; skip if ensemble confidence < 55%

low_confidence_ensemble.name: Low Confidence Single (Ambiguous Setup) - Ensemble Consensus Wins
low_confidence_ensemble.description: When individual signal confidence < 50%, ensemble consensus filtering (requiring 3+ agents agree) superior to trusting weak single signal
conditions: Individual signal confidence 25-50%
Setup ambiguous (multiple interpretations possible)
Regime weak/unclear
Price action indecisive
signal_examples: Trade agent says GO at 45% confidence (weak directional case)
Price forming inside bar; unclear breakout direction
Oscillators neutral, not at extremes
why_ensemble_beats_single: Ensemble requires consensus (3+ agents); weeds out weak signals
Ensemble confidence typically higher for false signals: 40-50%
Single weak signal = high false positive + low profit when right
Ensemble filtering dramatically reduces noise trades
low_confidence_ensemble.decision_rule: If single signal < 50% confidence, SKIP or wait for ensemble; don't force weak trades

multi_timeframe_ensemble.name: Multi-Timeframe Confirmation (Ensemble Alignment)
multi_timeframe_ensemble.description: When 5m, 1h, 4h, daily all aligned (ensemble framework), win rate significantly higher than any single timeframe
conditions: All timeframes showing same directional bias (5m + 1h + 4h + daily all bullish or all bearish)
Confluence across timeframes (moving averages aligned, momentum aligned)
Entry on lower timeframe with higher timeframe confirmation
signal_examples: Daily trend up, 4h trend up, 1h pullback to MA, 5m breakout; enter 5m with daily context
All timeframes in range, bounce off support visible on all; strong bounce trade
why_ensemble_beats_single: Multi-timeframe ensemble = true ensemble (different timeframes = different 'agents')
Alignment across timeframes provides structural confirmation
Single timeframe alone prone to whipsaws; multi-TF filters out false breaks
multi_timeframe_ensemble.decision_rule: Multi-timeframe alignment = best risk/reward; maximize position size

crisis_event_ensemble.name: Crisis/Panic Events (Ensemble Risk Management Dominates)
crisis_event_ensemble.description: During panics/crisis, ensemble risk agent's capital preservation logic often superior to single signal's profit-seeking
conditions: Market in panic regime (ATR 2x+, volume 3x+, gap move)
Individual signal gives GO (fade panic); Risk agent gives VETO (too risky)
Circuit breaker or drawdown limit approaching
Leverage already elevated
signal_examples: Crash happening; Trade agent wants to buy dip at 85% confidence; Risk agent vetoes (margin risk too high)
Daily loss at 1.8%; next trade would violate 2% limit; Critic agent says SKIP (portfolio risk)
why_ensemble_beats_single: Single signal ignores portfolio risk; ensemble veto prevents catastrophic loss
Risk agent + Critic agent provide double veto on dangerous trades
Ensemble drawdown management superior by 10pp
Psychological: Sitting out bad trades is often best trade
crisis_event_ensemble.decision_rule: Always defer to ensemble veto during crisis; survival > profits

regime_transition_ensemble.name: Regime Transition (Ensemble Detects, Single Misses)
regime_transition_ensemble.description: Ensemble framework detects regime transitions 1-4 hours before single-strategy trader; provides early warning
conditions: Multiple indicators showing regime shift (ADX rising, ATR changing, momentum shifting)
Regime agent flagging transition probability increasing
Single strategy still in old regime mode (e.g., mean reversion in beginning trend)
signal_examples: Range regime ending; ADX < 20 → 25 → 30 over 2 hours; Ensemble detects, adjusts parameters; single strategy gets whipsawed
Panic abating; ATR falling, volume declining; Ensemble recognizes stabilization; single panic trader gets caught in reversal
why_ensemble_beats_single: Ensemble has regime agent monitoring continuously
Single strategy locked into one mode; doesn't adapt
Ensemble parameter adjustments happen automatically
Regime agent consensus = early warning system
regime_transition_ensemble.decision_rule: Trust ensemble regime classification; adjust when regime confidence crosses 60% for new regime

statistical_ensemble.name: Statistical Averaging (Ensemble Risk Reduction)
statistical_ensemble.description: Even without special signal, ensemble averaging reduces variance (statistical benefit); lower drawdowns, more consistent returns
conditions: All agents generating signals (not much conflict)
Individual signal confidence 50-70% (moderate conviction)
No clear dominant strategy
signal_examples: Average of trend + mean reversion + momentum = averaged conviction, lower variance
5 traders voting differently; consensus = middle ground
why_ensemble_beats_single: Statistical averaging reduces variance without giving up much profit
Ensemble returns more consistent (lower drawdowns)
Sharpe ratio improvement significant (0.3+ points)
Better for long-term wealth accumulation
statistical_ensemble.decision_rule: Use ensemble for normal trading; leverage single signal only on exceptional setups

conflict_type_1.conflict: Trade agent GO vs Risk agent VETO
conflict_type_1.description: Trade agent wants trade; Risk agent says margin/capital risk too high
conflict_type_1.resolution_rule: Risk agent VETO always honored; reduce position size or skip
conflict_type_1.confidence_threshold: Go requires: 85%+ confidence from Trade agent + Risk agent approval
conflict_type_1.example: Trade agent: 'GO 80% confidence'; Risk agent: 'Margin = 18% of account, can't add more'; Resolution: SKIP or reduce size 50%

conflict_type_2.conflict: Trend agent GO vs Regime agent 'not aligned'
conflict_type_2.description: Trend agent bullish; Regime agent says 'not confident in trend yet'
conflict_type_2.resolution_rule: Require Regime agent >= 60% confidence in trend before following trend signal
conflict_type_2.confidence_threshold: Both agents must be aligned (Regime >=60% confidence + Trend GO)
conflict_type_2.example: Trend: 'Clear uptrend'; Regime: 'Only 40% confident in trend, could be range'; Resolution: REDUCE size, wait for Regime confidence

conflict_type_3.conflict: Critic agent VETO vs Trade agent GO
conflict_type_3.description: Trade agent confident; Critic agent flags counter-thesis that invalidates trade
conflict_type_3.resolution_rule: Critic VETO = skip trade; Critic's counter-thesis must be validated
conflict_type_3.confidence_threshold: Critic VETO only honored if counter-thesis >= 70% confidence
conflict_type_3.example: Trade: 'Breakout GO'; Critic: 'Counter-thesis: support may break below (70% confidence)'; Resolution: SKIP until support holds

conflict_type_4.conflict: Single strategy signal vs Ensemble consensus
conflict_type_4.description: One strategy bullish; other 3 neutral/bearish; majority says wait
conflict_type_4.resolution_rule: Ensemble consensus beats single strategy (unless single is 90%+ confidence + exceptional setup)
conflict_type_4.confidence_threshold: Single strategy >= 90% confidence + 4+ confluences to override ensemble
conflict_type_4.example: Trend: 'BUY 90%, textbook setup'; Momentum, Mean reversion, Regime: 'Not aligned'; Resolution: Can trade (high conviction), but reduce size 20%

conflict_type_5.description: Trailing stop agent says HOLD; Early exit agent says EXIT
conflict_type_5.resolution_rule: Take partial profit (50% on early exit signal, trail 50%)
conflict_type_5.confidence_threshold: Early exit requires 70%+ confidence in reversal signal
conflict_type_5.example: Trailing: 'Trail, momentum still strong'; Early exit: 'Divergence detected, 75% reversal confidence'; Resolution: TAKE 50%, trail 50%

decision_tree_logic.step_1: Calculate individual signal confidence; calculate ensemble consensus confidence
decision_tree_logic.step_2: If individual confidence >= 80% AND setup matches historical precedent AND regime aligned: CONSIDER SINGLE
decision_tree_logic.step_3: If individual confidence < 50% OR setup ambiguous: DEFAULT TO ENSEMBLE
decision_tree_logic.step_4: If ensemble confidence >= 55% AND multiple agents agree (3+): USE ENSEMBLE
decision_tree_logic.step_5: If conflict detected: Check Risk agent veto (always honored); Check Critic counter-thesis (honored if >=70% confidence)
decision_tree_logic.step_6: Calculate position size: confidence-based multiplier * regime-based multiplier * account-risk-based multiplier
decision_tree_logic.step_7: Final check: Verify no portfolio risk limits breached; execute

validation_rules.rule_1: Single signal >= 80% confidence required to override ensemble < 55% consensus
validation_rules.rule_2: Single signal setup must have >= 4 confluences to qualify as 'high conviction'
validation_rules.rule_3: Ensemble consensus requires >= 3 agents agreement (not just 2)
validation_rules.rule_4: Risk agent veto ALWAYS honored; no position size can exceed its approval
validation_rules.rule_5: Critic counter-thesis VETO only honored if counter-thesis confidence >= 70%
validation_rules.rule_6: Regime alignment required: Single signal regime confidence must be >= 60%
validation_rules.rule_7: Multi-timeframe ensemble (5m+1h+4h+daily aligned) automatically uses full Kelly sizing
validation_rules.rule_8: Conflicting signals within same agent (e.g., Exit agent disagree) = take partial profit strategy
validation_rules.rule_9: Crisis regime (panic detected) = reduce single signal sizing by 40%, increase ensemble weight
validation_rules.rule_10: Winning single signals tracked weekly; if accuracy drops below 60% over 20 samples, revert to ensemble
